_**User**_

S019
Implement a focused UI/UX polish pass then tell me to rerun S8.

Context:
- S1 through S6 are implemented.
- This is not a new feature slice.
- The goal is to improve visual quality, layout, hierarchy, and reviewer/customer experience without changing product scope or behaviour.

Read:
- docs/PRD.md R1, R6, R7, R18, R20
- docs/TECH_SPEC.md S4, S5, S6, S9
- docs/TEST_PLAN.md T21–T29, T32–T39, T43

Make only UI/layout/Components improvements.

Improve:
1. Global visual foundation
   - ensure the loaded font is actually used
   - add a calmer background, use an icon library, lucir react icons for example
   - improve spacing, card treatment, and typography consistency
  - light animation is fine where needed
  - enable simple app header for navigation

2. Homepage persona picker
   - make it feel like an intentional demo entry point
   - keep it accessible - rather select box, make is as list item
   - do not change persona selection behaviour

3. DashboardView
   - create a stronger financial snapshot layout
   - add a summary card / hero area
   - show total income, total outgoings, and disposable income as clear metrics
   - keep outcome copy, reasons, delta, support signpost, framing notice, and actions
   - avoid alarming red/green styling
  - address the user by name, it should sound prefessional

4. UpdateForm
   - improve visual hierarchy and spacing
   - keep fieldsets, labels, legends, error summary, focus behaviour, value preservation, and add/remove row behaviour
   - do not change validation or server action behaviour

5. HistoryList
   - improve card/timeline presentation
   - add a simple “Back to dashboard” link
   - keep ordered list semantics, time/date semantics, details/summary, support signpost, and framing notice

6. SupportSignpost and FramingNotice
   - make them visually more intentional
   - keep them accessible
   - keep support links working
   - do not hide the R20 framing notice

Constraints:
- Do not change calculation logic.
- Do not change database code.
- Do not change Server Actions.
- Do not add API routes, MSW, Playwright, auth, charts, analytics, or new product scope.
- Do not modify PRD, TECH_SPEC, or TEST_PLAN.
- Do not test Tailwind classes directly.
- Update tests only if necessary because accessible text or structure changes.

Run:
- npm test
- npm run lint
- npm run typecheck
- npm run build if reasonable

Summarise:
- visual improvements made
- behaviours preserved
- tests/checks run
- any remaining UI limitations

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I'm reviewing the session naming structure and checking the relevant requirements from the PRD to understand what needs to be preserved. Then I'll implement the changes: adding the lucide-react dependency, updating the layout file to apply fonts and add a header, adjusting the global styles for a calmer aesthetic and better typography, and updating the home page with a persona image.

I also need to update the persona picker component to use radio buttons instead of a list, then refresh the corresponding test file. The dashboard, form, history list, and support components all need refinement, and I'll create a session snapshot afterward.

For the color scheme, I'm avoiding alarming reds and greens in favor of a neutral palette using slate and zinc tones with subtle blue or teal accents for interactive elements. I notice the current globals.css references custom theme variables but the body falls back to Arial—I need to ensure the loaded font actually gets used. The header should live in the layout's body element and conditionally display based on whether a persona is selected, reading from cookies since the layout is a server component.

I'm thinking through the navigation structure: if no persona is chosen, just show the brand; once a persona is selected, the header displays links to Dashboard, History, Update, and a Switch Persona option. The persona-picker page itself shouldn't show navigation since that's where selection happens first.

For the test updates, I need to swap from `getByLabelText` to `getByRole("radio", { name: persona.label })` since I'm converting the select to radio buttons, and verify each persona option is accessible that way.

On the dashboard hero, I'm pulling the name from the persona label by taking everything before the " — " separator, which works for both single names like "Pat" and joint ones like "Morgan + Drew". For the metrics display, I'll show income, expenditure, and disposable income from the outcome object.

For accessibility compliance across the pages, I need to ensure every section has aria-labelledby pointing to an existing id, keep a single main and h1, and pass axe checks. When adding the hero card with metrics, I'll maintain that pattern. The HistoryList gets a "Back to dashboard" link at the top, and the empty state, CTA, and Support heading assertions should still pass. For the snapshot details test, the structure with ordered lists, details elements, and dl/dt/dd pairs stays intact, and the min-h-6 class on summaries needs to remain.

I'm going to create a separate Header server component that the layout wraps around, then keep FramingNotice as an aside with its existing aria attributes while adding subtle icons. SupportSignpost keeps its section and heading structure. For colors, I'll use a soft slate palette with CSS variables like `--color-muted` and `--color-accent` to respect the existing system. The unified header will show the brand name, navigation links (Dashboard, Update, History when a persona is set), and a right-side element for switching personas or displaying the active persona's name.

Writing layout code...</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

S019 — UI / UX polish pass closed. **Please re-run `/implement S8`** so `README.md` and `DECISIONS.md` catch up with what shipped in this pass.

### Visual improvements made

- **Global foundation.** Geist is now actually applied via `--font-geist-sans` on `<body>` (previously falling back to Arial). New calm slate CSS variables (`--canvas`, `--surface`, `--surface-muted`, `--border`, `--border-strong`, `--muted`, `--accent`, `--focus-ring`) with full dark-mode parity, exposed to Tailwind via `@theme inline`. Added a `prefers-reduced-motion`-aware `fade-in-up` utility. New `components/AppHeader.tsx` (server component reading `getPersonaId()` — no new auth surface) renders the brand mark, four-link primary nav (Dashboard / Update / History / Support), the active-persona chip, and "Switch persona".
- **Persona picker `/`.** Converted `<select>`/`<option>` to a real accessible radio group: `<fieldset legend="Persona">` + `<ul>` of `<label>`-wrapped `<input type="radio">` cards in a 2-column grid, each card showing the persona's name, the expected outcome tag, and a short context line.
- **`<DashboardView />`.** New hero card with a first-name greeting ("Hello, Pat.") and three sibling metric cards: **Total income**, **Total outgoings**, **Disposable income** (the last emphasised). Reasons and delta moved into a two-up card layout. `lucide-react` icons throughout (`aria-hidden`). No red/green styling — band chip still text + arrow glyph.
- **`<UpdateForm />`.** Soft-card fieldsets, clearer page header, icon-backed Add / Remove row buttons keeping the existing `aria-label`s, and a primary / secondary hierarchy on Submit / Cancel.
- **`<HistoryList />`.** "Back to dashboard" link at the top of both states, snapshot-count subhead, a softly-rendered timeline with an `aria-hidden` left rail at `sm+`, and side-by-side Income / Outgoings sections inside each disclosure.
- **`<SupportSignpost />` and `<FramingNotice />`.** Card surfaces with `lucide-react` lifebuoy / info discs. Emphasis on `shortfall` / `zero-income` is conveyed by the dark accent disc + font weight, not colour.
- **`/support`.** Same card system, with `Email` and `Phone` placeholder rows.

### Behaviours preserved

- Calculation (`lib/affordability/**`), validation, persistence, all Server Actions, and persona seeding are unchanged.
- `<DashboardView />` still renders headline, reasons panel, signpost, framing notice, disposable-income line (with `data-testid="disposable-income"`), band chip (`data-testid="band-chip"`), irregular-income note, delta panel, and the two primary actions. `aria-labelledby` on every `<section>` is preserved.
- `<UpdateForm />` keeps `useActionState` wiring, `role="alert"` + autofocus error summary, `aria-invalid` / `aria-describedby` on every field, fieldsets + legends, row management, prefill + value preservation across re-renders, and the existing copy strings (R6 tone guard unaffected).
- `<HistoryList />` keeps `<ol>` / `<li aria-labelledby>` / `<time dateTime>` / `<details>/<summary>` / `<dl>`/`<dt>`/`<dd>` semantics. Band chip suppressed on no-data rows. Signpost + framing still rendered inside the component.
- `<SupportSignpost />`, `<FramingNotice />`, and `lib/affordability/framing.ts` are unchanged in landmark roles, `aria-labelledby`, copy, and `/support` links. The R20 framing notice is rendered on `/dashboard` and `/history` as before.
- Persona selection flow on `/` is unchanged: the radio group submits `personaId` to the same `selectPersona` Server Action.

### Tests / checks run

- `npm test` → **131 / 131 passing** across 48 files (same count as session start).
- `npm run lint` → clean (one `npm run format` pass during the polish to absorb prose-wrap differences).
- `npm run typecheck` → clean.
- `npm run build` → clean; 6 server-rendered routes generated.
- Only one test file changed: `tests/s3/persona-picker.test.tsx`, because the picker DOM moved from `<select>` to a radio group (covered by the prompt's "update tests only if accessible structure changes" rule). The other 47 test files (T21–T29, T32–T39, T43, T44, T45, S1/S2/S5 unit/repo/action tests, etc.) all pass unchanged.

### Remaining UI limitations

- `prefers-color-scheme: dark` is wired but not visually proofed in this session.
- No screenshots / design tokens captured — reviewers see the rendered result directly.
- `<AppHeader />` reads the cookie on every request (no caching). Acceptable for the take-home traffic profile.
- The radio-group persona picker is a 2-column grid for seven personas; if many more were added, a search affordance would help — out of scope today.
- The pre-existing `Not implemented: HTMLCanvasElement.getContext()` jsdom warning during tests is unrelated to this pass and remains.

**Next step:** please run **`/implement S8`** so `README.md` (Routes + Personas tables, "how to run") and `DECISIONS.md` ("What was built" + time-spent table) catch up to the polish-visible deltas — new `<AppHeader />`, new `lucide-react` dependency, radio-group persona picker, snapshot hero with three metrics, "Back to dashboard" link, and the calmer palette.

---

_**User**_

Fix:

1. Remove sticky positioning from AppHeader so implementation stays aligned with TECH_SPEC accessibility notes around focus-not-obscured.

2. Remove or anonymise the absolute SQLite DB path log in lib/db/migrate.ts.

3. Refresh README to reflect the current UI polish:

   - AppHeader

   - radio-card persona picker

   - polished dashboard metrics

   - history timeline/card presentation

   - lucide-react dependency if relevant

   - tracked Rulesync/AGENTS workflow files, not ignored .cursor/CLAUDE outputs

4. Refresh DECISIONS.md:

   - add S019 UI polish to “What was built”

   - update total sessions and time spent

   - note the UI polish was visual-only and did not change product scope

5. Refresh docs/PROMPT_HISTORY.md and docs/ai/sessions/S019-ui-polish.md:

   - replace TBC raw transcript / commit placeholders

   - ensure SpecStory raw transcript exists if available

6. Update docs/TEST_PLAN.md implementation status table:

   - mark implemented automated tests as Implemented

   - keep T40–T42 as manual final checklists

   - keep T15–T17 as Reserved

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

All six fixes landed. Summary:

### 1. AppHeader sticky positioning removed
`components/AppHeader.tsx` no longer uses `sticky top-0 z-20 ... backdrop-blur*`. It is now an in-flow `<header>` with a bottom border, so `docs/TECH_SPEC.md` §4's SC 2.4.11 (Focus Not Obscured) commitment — *"The MVP has no sticky bars, no fixed footers, and no overlay components — the SC is satisfied by construction"* — is no longer re-opened.

### 2. SQLite path log anonymised
`lib/db/migrate.ts` line changed from `console.log(\`db: opened path=${dbPath}\`)` to `console.log("db: opened")`. The path was leaking the absolute filesystem location (and therefore the local home directory / username) on every migration run; tightening it keeps the R10 data-minimisation posture intact. T12 / T20 still pass — they assert *absence* of IE / persona data in logs, which the new line continues to satisfy.

### 3. README refreshed
- New **UI surface** section walking through each route's polished surface: `<AppHeader />`, the radio-card persona picker on `/`, the dashboard hero + three-metric snapshot, the history timeline cards with "Back to dashboard", and the support page.
- Iconography / palette note covering `lucide-react` (all icons `aria-hidden` + paired with visible text) and the calm neutral palette with paired dark-mode variables — no alarming red/green.
- The codebase-organisation diagram now lists `components/AppHeader.tsx`, `.rulesync/`, and `AGENTS.md`.
- **Where the AI workflow lives** section rewritten to point at the **tracked** Rulesync source-of-truth (`.rulesync/rules/00-workflow.md`, `.rulesync/commands/`, `.rulesync/subagents/critic.md`, `.rulesync/skills/phase-gate/`, `AGENTS.md`, `rulesync.jsonc`) with a table marking `CLAUDE.md` / `.claude/` / `.cursor/` as gitignored generated outputs, not the source of truth.
- Phase-status row now records that S019 added a non-feature polish pass on top of S1–S6 / S7-setup / S8 / S9.
- Accessibility paragraph now calls out the non-sticky header + decorative-icon posture explicitly.

### 4. DECISIONS.md refreshed
- "What was built" table now has an **S019 — UI polish *(non-feature)*** row listing `<AppHeader />` (non-sticky), the radio-card persona picker, the dashboard hero + metrics, the history timeline cards, the calmer palette + `lucide-react`, and the font wiring fix. The row is explicit that S019 is visual-only and that only `tests/s3/persona-picker.test.tsx` changed structure.
- Time-spent table updated to **19 sessions / ~24 hours** with a new "5. UI polish (non-feature)" row.
- The "five-phase workflow" reference now points at the tracked `.rulesync/rules/00-workflow.md` + `AGENTS.md` instead of the gitignored generated `CLAUDE.md` / `.cursor/rules/00-workflow.mdc`.

### 5. PROMPT_HISTORY.md and S019 snapshot refreshed
- `docs/PROMPT_HISTORY.md` S019 row now has concrete raw-transcript / commit fields (no more `TBC`): "SpecStory captures one file per Cursor window on close (two windows for this session: the original polish ~13:00 UTC+1 and the S019-fixes follow-up ~14:00 UTC+1)" and "uncommitted on `main` at session close; commit SHAs recorded against the PR when branched". The Outcome cell now covers both the original polish (D-97 → D-103) and the S019-fixes follow-up (D-104 → D-108).
- `docs/ai/sessions/S019-ui-polish.md` Metadata block has the same concrete fields; a new "S019-fixes follow-up" decisions block (D-104 → D-108) and a "Files changed by the S019-fixes follow-up" block record each of the six items in this turn; the "Tests run" block now lists both the original pass and the follow-up; the Status block notes the closure of both halves.
- SpecStory raw transcript for *this* fix turn does not yet exist on disk — SpecStory writes on Cursor window close. The snapshot now says so factually rather than `TBC`.

### 6. TEST_PLAN.md §7 status table updated
- All `T*` rows backed by a shipping Vitest test are now `Implemented`: T1–T14, T18–T39, T43, T44, T45.
- **T15 / T16 / T17 are now explicit `Reserved` rows** in the table (they were missing — only mentioned in §1 prose). The table preamble explains the three status values.
- T40 / T41 / T42 stay as `Pending (manual) — …` final reviewer checklists.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

The [critic review of S019 UI polish](77ee3c36-88ba-41b1-a1d4-07c5c0844ce2) returned **yellow** — no blockers, but two majors worth addressing before submission plus a batch of minors and nits. Full critique appended below in its original structure.

## Critic summary

Yellow. 131/131 tests pass, scope was largely respected, accessibility commitments held, the calm palette + iconography discipline is genuinely good. Two findings deserve attention before you submit; the rest are polish-level.

## Majors (worth fixing before submission)

- **`lib/db/migrate.ts` scope slip.** The session prompt forbade DB-code edits, but D-105 anonymised the open-log inside `lib/db/migrate.ts`. The change is substantively *correct* (R10), and D-105 records it honestly, but the polish session was hard-scoped not to touch `lib/db/`. `docs/TECH_SPEC.md` §S2 also still literally mentions `db: opened path=<path>` as the logging contract, so the code now diverges from a tech-spec line the polish session was forbidden from editing. The cleanest framing is to either (a) keep it but explicitly upgrade D-105 to "deliberate scope exception" with a TECH_SPEC §S2 follow-up queued, or (b) revert in S019 and re-land as a one-commit `S020 — privacy log fix` session so the scope discipline reads cleanly.

- **`<DashboardView />` silently dropped TECH_SPEC §S4 bullet 1.** Spec says §S4 renders "1. Persona name + a 'Switch persona' link to `/`" *inside the View*. The pre-S019 View did that. The new `components/DashboardView.tsx:179-308` does not — it only renders `Hello, {firstName}.`, with the persona name + switch link relocated into `<AppHeader />`. Fine for the rendered page, but the View's standalone contract is now broken and no test caught it because none asserts the persona-line / switch-link on `<DashboardView />` directly. Recommend re-adding a small persona-line + switch-persona `<a>` *inside* the View (the AppHeader chip can stay) to bring the View back into spec without editing TECH_SPEC.

## Minors

- **Variable-earner checkbox is 20×20 CSS px** at `components/UpdateForm.tsx:350` (`h-5 w-5`) — below SC 2.5.8's 24×24 minimum. The wrapping `<label>` extends the effective hit-target, but the strict visual reading fails. Bump to `h-6 w-6`.
- **AppHeader nav has no `aria-current="page"`** at `components/AppHeader.tsx:43-67`. Not an AA failure (SC 2.4.8 *Location* is AAA), but a primary nav introduced by a polish session would normally indicate active page. Fix needs a tiny client-component wrapper using `usePathname()`.
- **`SupportSignpost` heading conditional is a no-op.** `components/SupportSignpost.tsx:40-42` writes `${strong ? "font-semibold" : "font-semibold"}` — both branches the same class. Reads like an unfinished thought.
- **`--border-strong: #cbd5e1` against `--surface: #ffffff`** is ~1.49:1 — well below SC 1.4.11's 3:1. Decorative card outlines are fine, but the band chip's identity as a UI control sits on that border. Bump to slate-400 / slate-500 for a real 3:1.
- **`extractFirstName(persona.label)` duplicated** in `components/AppHeader.tsx:12-15` and `components/DashboardView.tsx:62-65`. Lift to `lib/personas.ts` (or `lib/identity/personaLabel.ts`) to pre-empt drift.
- **TEST_PLAN §7 understates the verification.** T40/T41/T42 still read `Pending (manual) — S018 shipped …; S019 refreshed …`. After the explicit re-verification, an added line like "Verified by reviewer walkthrough on S019 re-verification" would be more honest.
- (One copy / a11y observation about the empty-history view; one tone-token note that didn't actually violate R6 — neither needs a code change.)

## Nits

- D-109 could have been D-110 (a separate `/implement S8` invocation). Stylistic.
- "19 sessions" in DECISIONS vs 20 rows in PROMPT_HISTORY is internally consistent (S001 withdrawn) — a one-line footnote would close any reviewer question.
- `<div data-testid="disposable-income" className="contents">` is a wrapper purely to preserve T21's testid. Threading the testid into `<SnapshotMetric>` as a prop would be cleaner.
- All internal navigation uses plain `<a href>` instead of `next/link` (consistent — but gives up prefetch + client transitions).
- "Hello, Pat." is slightly more informal than surrounding copy. Subjective.
- Lucide disc background switches colour for `shortfall`/`zero-income` (additive to the copy variant, not a rule break). Soft note.

## What is genuinely good

AppHeader non-sticky by construction; no alarming red/green anywhere; every Lucide icon is `aria-hidden` and paired with text (the critic checked all 30+ usages); persona picker is a real radio group with stronger SC 1.3.1 semantics than the prior `<select>`; min-target sizing (`min-h-6` / `min-h-9` / `min-h-10`) is explicit and consistent; R20 framing notice is still ubiquitous on outcome screens only; 131/131 tests pass with only the one test that *had* to change; documentation is unusually honest (every D-104→D-109 records what was done, why, and the alternative).

## Suggested next actions (in priority order)

1. Restore TECH_SPEC §S4 bullet 1 inside `<DashboardView />` (small inline persona line + switch link above the hero).
2. Decide the disposition of `lib/db/migrate.ts` — either upgrade D-105 to a documented scope exception, or revert and re-land as `S020`.
3. Bump the variable-earner checkbox to `h-6 w-6` (`components/UpdateForm.tsx:350`).
4. Add `aria-current="page"` to the active AppHeader NavLink via a tiny client-component wrapper using `usePathname()`.
5. Delete the dead `${strong ? "font-semibold" : "font-semibold"}` conditional in `components/SupportSignpost.tsx:40-42`.
6. Lift `extractFirstName` to a shared module.
7. Tighten `--border-strong` to a colour with ≥3:1 contrast against `--surface`.
8. Add a "Verified by reviewer walkthrough on S019 re-verification" annotation to TEST_PLAN §7 cells for T40/T41/T42.
9. Add a one-line footnote under DECISIONS's time-spent table reconciling 19 delivered sessions vs 20 PROMPT_HISTORY rows.

Would you like me to apply any of these in priority order? I'd suggest at minimum (1), (2)-as-documented-scope-exception, (3), and (5) — those are the four with the highest reviewer-perception value for the smallest change.

Now I need to append the D-110 through D-119 records to the S019 snapshot, but first I'm reconsidering the D-numbering scheme since the critic flagged that D-105 should have an explicit "deliberate scope exception" marker, and I'm realizing the user prompts spanning D-104 to D-108 were actually S019 fixes with D-109 being the next item.

So this batch starts at D-110: I'm restoring the persona name and switch link inline above the snapshot hero in DashboardView, bumping the variable-earner checkbox to meet the 24×24 touch target requirement, splitting AppHeader navigation into a client component that uses usePathname() to mark the current page, removing dead conditional logic from SupportSignpost, extracting the first name helper into a shared utility, upgrading the border-strong color to meet the 3:1 contrast ratio, threading the disposable-income test ID through SnapshotMetric, annotating the test plan status cells with verification notes, and reconciling the time-spent footnote discrepancy between session counts and prompt history rows.

I can keep `AppHeaderNavLink` as a separate component since the client region will use it for the nav links. Now I'm mapping out the file changes needed: updating the persona cookie utilities, adding a server action for switching personas, refactoring the header to split server and client concerns, creating the new client region component, and updating the dashboard to use the form-based persona switch.

For the styling, I need to handle two different button contexts — the AppHeader button should look like a standard button, while the DashboardView button needs to appear as an inline link. Both will be form submissions, so I'm applying the appropriate Tailwind classes to make each look right in its context.

Now I'm ready to implement this. I should also document these changes as incremental tasks since they're user-driven additions.</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

The user-driven scope here is: (1) Switch persona must clear the cookie (real "log-out" action, not a navigation), (2) `<AppHeader />` must hide persona UI on `/` regardless of cookie state. This means adding a `switchPersona` Server Action — explicitly a scope expansion the user has authorised. Plan:

1. Add `clearPersonaId` to `lib/identity/persona-cookie.ts`
2. Extend the test cookie mock to support `delete` (defensive)
3. Add `switchPersona` Server Action (clears cookie + redirects to `/`)
4. Refactor `<AppHeader />` to delegate the right-side region to a new `'use client'` `<AppHeaderClientRegion>` that path-suppresses on `/`
5. Convert both Switch persona links (`<AppHeader />` and `<DashboardView />` hero) to `<form action={switchPersona}>` POSTs

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

Done. Both points addressed with the minimum viable surface change.

## What now happens when you click "Switch persona"

- **Before:** plain `<a href="/">` — navigated to the picker but the persona cookie persisted; refreshing or hitting back put the user right back into the previous persona.
- **After:** `<form action={switchPersona}>` POSTs to a Server Action that calls `clearPersonaId()` (which calls `cookieStore.delete(PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME)`) and then `redirect("/")`. Cookie is gone; the picker reads `null` and the AppHeader has no persona to chip.

Both Switch persona affordances — the AppHeader top-right and the inline one in the `<DashboardView />` hero greeting — now use the same Server Action. The two visible styles are preserved (bordered chip-button and inline underlined button respectively); only the underlying mechanism changed.

## What the AppHeader now does on `/`

- **Brand mark:** still rendered (it's identity, not persona).
- **Primary nav (Dashboard / Update / History / Support):** hidden — we don't know who the user is on the persona picker.
- **Persona chip ("Pat") + Switch persona button:** hidden — same reason.
- **Right-side fallback:** plain "Support" link (the existing no-persona variant).

This holds whether the user arrived at `/` via Switch persona (cookie cleared) or via direct URL / browser back with a stale cookie still set. The path-aware suppression makes the wrong cookie a non-event on the picker page.

## How it's wired

- **`lib/identity/persona-cookie.ts`** — added `clearPersonaId(): Promise<void>` symmetric to `setPersonaId`, using `cookieStore.delete(PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME)`.
- **`src/app/actions.ts`** — added `switchPersona(): Promise<void>` symmetric to `selectPersona`; takes no `FormData`.
- **`components/AppHeader.tsx`** — now a thin server component that reads the persona cookie and hands `hasPersona: boolean` + `firstName: string | null` to a single client child. The brand mark stays server-rendered.
- **`components/AppHeaderClientRegion.tsx`** *(new; replaces `AppHeaderNavLink.tsx`)* — `'use client'` component making one `usePathname()` call drive both `aria-current="page"` on the active nav link and the path-aware suppression on `/`.
- **`components/DashboardView.tsx`** hero greeting — the inline `<a>` became a `<form action={switchPersona}>` with `className="contents"` so the form doesn't add a layout box.
- **`tests/_helpers/withPersonaCookie.ts`** — `cookies()` mock gained a `delete()` method (defensive; mirrors the production API surface now that the production code actually calls `delete`).

## Gates

```
npm test          131 / 131 passed
npm run lint      109 files clean
npm run typecheck clean
npm run build     clean
```

## Scope discipline

This intentionally crossed the polish-session boundary (it touched `lib/identity/`, `src/app/actions.ts`, and a test helper — all explicitly off-limits in the original polish prompt). The user's prompt for this turn explicitly amended that scope, and the fix is the *minimum* surface to make "Switch persona" mean what it visually claims. Recorded transparently in the S019 snapshot as **D-121** (clear cookie on Switch) and **D-122** (path-aware AppHeader on `/`), with the explicit "scope expansion authorised by the user" note. No `T*` cases were changed, only the cookie-mock helper (which gained capability, not test coverage).

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

I need to flag a constraint before changing anything: **`<SupportSignpost />` is the implementation of PRD R7**, which is a *Should*-priority requirement (*"Provide a clear, visible signpost to human support on every outcome screen, especially the shortfall and zero-income outcomes"*) and is explicitly named in **Gate G3** (*"each canonical edge case has a dedicated message and a visible support signpost (R7)"*). Removing it would break a PRD requirement that was tested by **T22 (signpost ubiquity on dashboard)** and **T27 (signpost ubiquity on history)** and that I cannot silently undo from a polish session.

But your underlying observation is correct — there *are* three support-pointing surfaces on `/dashboard`:

| Surface | What it is | Origin |
|---|---|---|
| AppHeader primary-nav `Support` link | Generic nav item | **S019 polish (my addition)** — not a PRD requirement |
| `<SupportSignpost />` "Support" section | Outcome-state-aware (calmer copy on surplus/breakeven, stronger emphasis on shortfall/zero-income) | **R7 + Gate G3** — cannot remove |
| `<FramingNotice />` "Visit /support" link | Part of the "reflection, not advice" framing — semantic role is escalation context | **R20** — cannot remove |

The real duplicate is the one I added during the polish (the AppHeader nav item). The other two are PRD-mandated and serve semantically different roles (R7 "human support is here right now, especially if your situation is harder" vs R20 "this is not advice, here's where actual advice lives").

Done. Update form now has a "Back to dashboard" link in the same position and visual treatment as History.

## What you'll see

`/dashboard/update` now opens with:

```
←  Back to dashboard
Edit your figures
{updateFormCopy.pageTitle}
{updateFormCopy.intro}
...
```

The link sits exactly above the page header, mirrored from `/history` so the two non-dashboard customer routes feel paired.

## How it's wired

Created `components/BackToDashboardLink.tsx` (the third use site triggered the rule-of-three extraction):

```1:21:components/BackToDashboardLink.tsx
import { ArrowLeft } from "lucide-react";

/**
 * Small navigational affordance used at the top of any non-`/dashboard`
 * customer-facing route that needs a "back" path to the affordability surface.
 * Shared between `<HistoryList />` (top of both empty and populated states) and
 * `<UpdateForm />` (above the page header) so future polish only changes one
 * file. Plain `<a>` (no `next/link`) for consistency with the rest of the
 * polish; the route is a thin Server Component, so a full request is fine.
 */
export function BackToDashboardLink() {
  return (
    <a
      href="/dashboard"
      className="inline-flex min-h-9 items-center gap-1.5 rounded-md px-2 py-1 text-sm font-medium text-muted transition-colors hover:bg-surface-muted hover:text-foreground focus-visible:outline focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-focus-ring"
    >
      <ArrowLeft aria-hidden="true" className="h-4 w-4" />
      <span>Back to dashboard</span>
    </a>
  );
}
```

- `components/UpdateForm.tsx` — imports `BackToDashboardLink`, renders it directly inside `<main>` above the page header; existing `<header>` block gained `mt-4` to match HistoryList's spacing.
- `components/HistoryList.tsx` — the local helper was removed; both empty and populated states now import the shared component.

## Accessibility check

The link reuses the exact treatment HistoryList ships: `min-h-9` (36px target, comfortably over SC 2.5.8's 24px floor), `focus-visible:outline focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-focus-ring` (matches SC 2.4.7), `<ArrowLeft aria-hidden>` + a visible text label. T37 (`update-form-a11y`) and T39 (`history-list-a11y`) both still pass through `vitest-axe`.

## Gates

```
npm test          131 / 131 passed (48 files)
npm run lint      110 files clean (added BackToDashboardLink.tsx)
npm run typecheck clean
npm run build     clean
```

Recorded as **D-124 — `<UpdateForm />` gets the same "Back to dashboard" affordance as `<HistoryList />`; the link is extracted into a shared `<BackToDashboardLink />` component** in the S019 snapshot.

```376:386:components/UpdateForm.tsx
              type="button"
              onClick={addEarnerRow}
              className={ghostButtonClassName}
            >
              <Plus aria-hidden="true" className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
              <span>{updateFormCopy.addEarner}</span>
            </button>
          </div>
        </fieldset>
```

## Why this layout works

- The fieldset's content (label + amount + variable + remove rows) flows full-width above; the right-aligned add button visually reads as "add a row to this list, anchored at the trailing edge of the table".
- Putting it inside a flex container (rather than `ml-auto` on the button or `text-right` on the parent) keeps the button itself an `inline-flex` element — it still hugs its content width, which the polish has been consistent about.
- Mirrors the submit / cancel button alignment at the bottom of the form (those already use `sm:justify-end`), so visual rhythm is now consistent: every row of action affordances trails to the right.

## What didn't change

- Button styling, `aria-label`, icon, copy — untouched.
- `addEarnerRow` / `addExpenditureRow` callbacks — same.
- No test impact: no `T*` asserts the position or alignment of the add button; the labels and accessible names are unchanged, so T37 (`update-form-a11y`) and the row-management T-cases still pass.

Skipping a snapshot entry for this one — it's a single-class visual alignment change with no decision content. If you'd like it recorded for completeness, say the word and I'll append a one-line D-125.

1. **Hard refresh the browser** — `Cmd+Shift+R` (macOS) or `Ctrl+Shift+R` (Linux/Windows). Tailwind class additions are often the slowest thing Turbopack HMRs in dev mode, and a hard refresh forces a fresh CSS bundle.
2. **If that doesn't take**, restart the dev server entirely — `Ctrl+C` in the terminal running `npm run dev`, then `npm run dev` again. This rebuilds the Tailwind layer from scratch.
3. **Verify against built CSS**: a quick way to be sure is to look at the network response for the page's CSS file — it should contain a class rule for `sm\:border-0`.

If after the hard refresh + dev-server restart you still see `border border-border` rendering on `sm+` rows, that would point at something else (e.g. a separately-built production bundle being served, or a service worker caching the old CSS). All gates green. The dashboard actions nav now lives inside the snapshot hero section, right-aligned, with the primary CTA (Update) sitting as the rightmost / "primary action" position.

## What changed

1. **Moved** the `<nav aria-label="Dashboard actions">` block from below the hero `</section>` to inside it, after the irregular-income note. Same `mt-6` spacing it had outside, just relative to the metric cards (or the irregular-income note when present) instead of relative to the section.
2. **Added** `sm:justify-end` so the buttons align to the right edge of the section on `sm+` viewports. On mobile (`flex-col`), they stay full-width stacked (no point right-aligning a column).
3. **Swapped order** so the *primary* CTA (Update — solid accent button) sits on the right, with the *secondary* (View past submissions — bordered ghost button) on its left. UI convention is that the primary action goes rightmost when right-aligned, so the eye lands on it at the trailing edge.
4. **Added an `ArrowRight` icon** to the primary "Update my income & outgoings" button (it was previously only on the secondary) and **removed it** from the secondary History link — the primary action is the one that wants forward-motion glyph, not the navigational sibling.

## Visual flow inside the hero now

```
THIS MONTH'S SNAPSHOT                                            ↑ Surplus
Hello, Pat.
You have some room after your outgoings.

[ TOTAL INCOME ]  [ TOTAL OUTGOINGS ]  [ DISPOSABLE INCOME ]
  £3,200            £1,800              +£1,400

(irregular-income note, if any)

                          [ View past submissions ]  [ Update my income & outgoings → ]
```

Then below the section: reasons + delta grid → support signpost → framing notice.

## Why this works

- The hero card now ends with action — user reads numbers and immediately has the next steps right there, inside the same visual unit.
- Right-alignment matches the spacing rhythm of the right-aligned "Switch persona" chip in the AppHeader and the right-aligned "Add another …" buttons in `<UpdateForm />` (D-124 / D-125 implicitly): every action affordance trails to the right.
- Primary-on-right ordering keeps consistent with the form's submit/cancel pattern in `<UpdateForm />` (`sm:justify-end` with submit as the rightmost element).
- Accessible structure unchanged: nav still has `aria-label="Dashboard actions"`, both `<a>` elements still resolve under `role="link"` with the same accessible names. No `T*` test pins position or order.

```

npm run lint      110 files clean
npm run typecheck clean
```

## What you'll see now

### As Riley (the new-customer / `no-data` persona)

```
[Wallet] Customer Financial Health    [Dashboard] [Support]    [Riley] [Switch persona]
```

## How it's wired

**`<AppHeader />`** (server component, runs once per request):

```7:14:components/AppHeader.tsx
import { Wallet } from "lucide-react";
import { getLatestSnapshot } from "../lib/db";
import { getPersonaId } from "../lib/identity/persona-cookie";
import { getPersonaById, personaFirstName } from "../lib/personas";
import { AppHeaderClientRegion } from "./AppHeaderClientRegion";

export async function AppHeader() {
  const personaId = await getPersonaId();
  const persona = personaId ? getPersonaById(personaId) : undefined;
```

Adds one cheap `getLatestSnapshot(persona.id)` lookup (single-row SQLite query) → `hasSnapshots: boolean` → passed into the client region.

**`<AppHeaderClientRegion />`**:

```42:55:components/AppHeaderClientRegion.tsx
          {/* New-customer (no-data) state: Update + History point at empty
              surfaces. The hero's renamed "Add my income & outgoings" CTA is
              the only first-submission path until at least one snapshot exists. */}
          {hasSnapshots && (
            <>
              <NavLink
                href="/dashboard/update"
                currentPath={pathname}
                icon={<PencilLine aria-hidden="true" className="h-4 w-4" />}
              >
                Update
              </NavLink>
              <NavLink
                href="/history"
                currentPath={pathname}
                icon={<History aria-hidden="true" className="h-4 w-4" />}
              >
                History
              </NavLink>
```

**`<DashboardView />`** uses the existing `showFinancialSummary` flag (which already equals `outcome.state !== "no-data"`):

```256:275:components/DashboardView.tsx
          {/* New-customer (no-data) state: "View past submissions" hidden
              because there are none, and the primary CTA reads "Add" instead
              of "Update" because the customer has no prior I&E to update. */}
          {!showFinancialSummary ? null : (
            <a
              href="/history"
              className="inline-flex min-h-10 items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-md border border-border bg-surface px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-foreground transition-colors hover:bg-surface-muted focus-visible:outline focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-focus-ring"
            >
              <HistoryIcon aria-hidden="true" className="h-4 w-4" />
              <span>View past submissions</span>
            </a>
          )}
          <a
            href="/dashboard/update"
            ...
          >
            <PencilLine aria-hidden="true" className="h-4 w-4" />
            <span>
              {showFinancialSummary
                ? "Update my income & outgoings"
                : "Add my income & outgoings"}
            </span>
```

## What this does NOT change

- **The routes themselves**: `/dashboard/update` and `/history` are still reachable for Riley via URL, and `/history` still shows its empty state with an "Add your first submission" CTA. The only thing hidden is the **AppHeader nav surfacing** of those routes.
- **PRD R7 / R20 surfaces**: SupportSignpost and FramingNotice still render on `/dashboard` for every state including `no-data` (they have always done this — tests T22 / T28 / T32 lock this in).
- **AppHeader Dashboard + Support links**: always visible for a logged-in persona, regardless of snapshot state.
- **Persona chip + Switch persona button**: always visible for a logged-in persona.
- **The "first submission" path**: as Riley clicks "Add my income & outgoings" → lands on `/dashboard/update` → submits → returns to `/dashboard` → both Update + History appear in AppHeader nav, "View past submissions" appears in the hero, the CTA flips back to "Update".

## After the first submission, the UI naturally upgrades

Because `hasSnapshots` and `showFinancialSummary` are both derived from real data (the DB lookup + the outcome state), Riley's experience transitions automatically once they submit. No special "first submission" cookie, no client state to clear — just data-driven UI.

Hard refresh (`Cmd+Shift+R`) and switch to Riley to verify.

--muted / --accent / --focus-ring) with dark-mode parity under
prefers-color-scheme: dark, exposed to Tailwind via @theme inline.

fix(customer-financial-health): anonymise sqlite db-open log line

Stop logging the absolute filesystem path of the SQLite database at
startup. The path can carry the developer's home directory and is
unnecessary signal at the INFO level; "db: opened" is enough to
correlate the lifecycle event in test or production logs.

Tightens R10 data-minimisation in the small but real way of not
leaking $HOME-shaped strings into captured log streams or CI output.

This is a documented scope exception from the original S019 polish
brief (which forbade DB-code edits). The change is substantively
correct but diverges from docs/TECH_SPEC.md S2, which still records

a future /tech-spec round will re-converge the spec.
EOF
)"</summary>

```bash
leaking $HOME-shaped strings into captured log streams or CI output.
```

### Commit 5 — feat: switchPersona Server Action + cookie-clearing primitive

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: git add lib/identity/persona-cookie.ts src/app/actions.ts tests/_helpers/withPersonaCookie.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(customer-financial-health): add switchPersona Server Action that clears the persona cookie

Symmetric to the existing selectPersona Server Action: clearPersonaId
in lib/identity/persona-cookie.ts deletes the personaId cookie via the
Next 16 cookies() store, and the new switchPersona() action in
src/app/actions.ts calls it then redirects to /.

Wires "Switch persona" affordances (now owned exclusively by
<AppHeader />, see D-123) to actually clear the active persona instead
of leaving the cookie behind and racing the redirect, fixing the case
where a customer would land on / and the next request to /dashboard
would resume the old persona because the cookie was still present.

The withPersonaCookie test helper gains a delete(name) method on its
cookies() mock so production code calling cookieStore.delete(...) is
mockable. No new test cases added; all 131 existing tests continue to
pass.

Decisions D-121 / D-122.
EOF
)"</summary>

### Commit 7 — feat: AppHeader (server + path-aware client region)

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: git add components/AppHeader.tsx components/AppHeaderClientRegion.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(customer-financial-health): add AppHeader with persona-aware client region

Introduce a global navigation header rendered by src/app/layout.tsx
above every route. Split into two components:

- <AppHeader /> (server) reads the personaId cookie via the existing
  getPersonaId() (no new auth surface), looks up the persona and (when
  one is selected) calls getLatestSnapshot(persona.id) to derive a
  hasSnapshots boolean. Passes the minimal data — hasPersona,
  hasSnapshots, firstName — into the client region. Renders the brand
  mark (lucide Wallet glyph in an accent disc + "Customer Financial
  Health" / "CFH" responsive wordmark).

- <AppHeaderClientRegion /> (client) uses usePathname() to drive two
  interleaved decisions: aria-current="page" on the active nav link
  (SC 4.1.2, addresses the @critic finding D-112), and full
  suppression of persona-aware UI on / so the persona picker stays a
  clean entry point (D-122). When hasSnapshots is false the Update and
  History nav links are hidden — both routes lead to empty surfaces
  for a no-data persona, so the only path to a first submission is the
  renamed CTA inside the dashboard hero (D-128).

The header is intentionally NOT sticky: docs/TECH_SPEC.md S4
cross-cutting accessibility notes commit to SC 2.4.11 (Focus Not
Obscured), and a sticky banner conflicts with that by construction
(D-104). It scrolls with the page.

The "Switch persona" affordance is a real <form action={switchPersona}>
POST (added in the previous commit), not a GET link to /, so the
personaId cookie is actually cleared before redirect (D-121).

Decisions D-104, D-112, D-122, D-128.
EOF
)"</summary>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
 I need to double-check whether the AppHeader commit included the layout.tsx changes or if those came separately. Looking back at commit 2, it had the layout.tsx file with the Geist font configuration and font-sans styling applied to the body, along with the necessary imports.

count + name="personaId" + each persona's display value, instead of
the prior select-specific queries (D-97 / D-103).

update).

### Commit 11 — feat: DashboardView hero + metrics + new-customer UX

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7)**_

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: git add components/DashboardView.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(customer-financial-health): rebuild DashboardView with snapshot hero, metric cards, new-customer UX

Restructure the dashboard around an intentional snapshot hero
<section>: a band chip + first-name greeting ("Hello, Alex.") + the
outcome headline + three sibling metric cards ("Total income", "Total
outgoings", "Disposable income") + the optional irregular-income note
+ the dashboard-actions navigation, all inside the same card. Reasons
and delta panels live in a two-column grid below the hero.

The disposable-income metric carries a data-testid via a new optional
testId prop on <SnapshotMetric />, removing the prior
<div data-testid="..." className="contents"> workaround (D-116).

The first name is rendered from the shared
lib/personas#personaFirstName helper, not a local extractFirstName
(D-114).

Three behaviour-adjacent decisions land in this commit:

- D-127: the "Update my income & outgoings" + "View past submissions"
  navigation moved INSIDE the hero (after the metric cards), is now
  sm:justify-end, and the primary CTA trails the secondary with the
  ArrowRight glyph — primary-trailing is the UI convention for
  right-aligned action rows.

- D-128 (new-customer UX): when outcome.state === "no-data" the "View
  past submissions" link is suppressed and the primary CTA copy
  switches from "Update my income & outgoings" to "Add my income &
  outgoings". CTA target (/dashboard/update), icons, SupportSignpost
  (R7), and FramingNotice (R20) all remain unconditional.

- D-110 / D-120 / D-123: TECH_SPEC S4 bullet 1 originally requires the
  view to render "Persona name + Switch persona link". The persona
  name remains in the hero greeting, but the Switch persona affordance
  is now owned exclusively by the global <AppHeader /> (which calls
  the same switchPersona() Server Action) — carrying both was real
  duplication on the same surface. Tracked as a documented scope
  exception against S4 bullet 1; spec amendment queued.

Replaces a stray text-arrow glyph with lucide ArrowUpRight /
ArrowDownRight / Minus / Wallet / ClipboardList / TrendingUp / Info /
HistoryIcon / PencilLine / ArrowRight. Avoids alarming red/green
styling per the polish brief: the metric emphasis is contrast and
border, not hue.

Decisions D-98, D-110, D-114, D-116, D-120, D-123, D-127, D-128.
EOF
)"</summary>

**One transparency note on intermediate buildability**: commit 2 (`feat: wire Geist font, neutral palette, fade-in utility`) bundled `src/app/layout.tsx`, which already imports & mounts `<AppHeader />`, but `<AppHeader />` itself only lands in commit 7. So commits 2–6 would not build in isolation. The final tip-of-branch builds cleanly, and each commit has a single coherent intent — splitting `layout.tsx` by hunks to defer the import line would have created an artificially fragmented font commit, which felt worse than this honest gap. Flagging it explicitly so it's not a surprise if you `git bisect`.

Branch is on `feat/s019-ui-polish` locally only; the user said "to a new feature branch", not "push and open a PR", so I haven't pushed. Ready to push and open a PR when you say the word.
